name: 21_Iris_Classification_SVM_Iris_ML
query: I request a project to classify iris species utilizing the Iris dataset with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier implemented in `src/model.py`. The project should standardize the data in and perform feature selection in `src/data_loader.py`. It will document the classification accuracy and save it as `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt`, and generate and save a confusion matrix as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. It will further create an interactive web application in `src/app.py` using Streamlit to showcase classification results and model performance, with the figures stored in `results/figures/`. The web page should be user-friendly, with a brief explanation of the model to help users understand how the SVM classifier works.

requirements.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.
Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.
A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

preferences.criteria: The Streamlit web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily explore different aspects of the model's performance.
A brief model explanation should be included on the web page, helping users understand how the SVM classifier works.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The project files contain only placeholder/stub implementations. The `data_loader.py` file has a `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual loading of the Iris dataset. There is no import of `sklearn.datasets.load_iris`, no reference to any Iris dataset file, and no actual data loading logic anywhere in the codebase. The `model.py` and `app.py` are similarly minimal stubs. The trajectory evidence also confirms no steps involving loading or processing the Iris dataset were found.

judge_stats.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a placeholder/stub implementation. The actual `load_dataset()` function simply returns a dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no real data loading, and there is no standardization logic whatsoever — no `StandardScaler`, `MinMaxScaler`, normalization routines, or any fit/transform calls. The file is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and does not implement actual data standardization to ensure feature values are within the same range.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual feature selection logic. The file contains only placeholder/stub code with a `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and a `build_transforms()` function returning a static list. There is no actual feature selection implementation (e.g., using `SelectKBest`, `PCA`, `feature_importances_`, or any other feature selection technique). The `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` list merely mentions feature selection as a string but does not implement it. The trajectory evidence also confirms no feature selection operations were performed in this file.

<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement an SVM classifier. Instead, it contains only a minimal placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder. There is no actual SVM implementation — no import of `sklearn.svm.SVC` or any other SVM library, no model training, no fitting logic, and no classifier functionality. The file merely lists "SVM classifier" as a keyword string in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS`, which is not an implementation.

<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` — this is clearly a placeholder value, not an actual classification accuracy metric. The file does not contain any real accuracy score (e.g., a numeric value like `0.9667` or a formatted accuracy report). The trajectory evidence also confirms that no classification accuracy was ever computed or saved to this file during execution. Therefore, while the file exists at the correct path, it does not contain valid classification accuracy data.

judge_stats.criteria: A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` exists in the workspace, as confirmed by the project tree structure which explicitly lists `results\figures\confusion_matrix.png`. While the image content could not be read due to a media type error in the evaluation tool (the file is a PNG but was processed as JPEG), the file's presence in the directory confirms it was generated and saved to the correct location. The workspace structure clearly shows the file exists at the expected path `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/app.py` file does not implement an actual Streamlit web application. Instead, it contains a minimal stub/placeholder that only defines some metadata strings and a `main()` function returning a dictionary. There is no Streamlit import (`import streamlit as st`), no Streamlit UI components (e.g., `st.title()`, `st.image()`, `st.write()`), and no code to display classification results or model performance figures from `results/figures/`. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a functional Streamlit application.
